summary: Iteration 17 — Backend-only verification of the user's REAL-TIME per-use deletion feature ('ek line use hoe wo sath he delete ho jay'). Created /app/backend/tests/test_iteration17_per_use_deletion.py with 5 isolated tests against an ephemeral TEST_iter17_<hex> user (registered via /api/auth/register, features enabled by admin via PUT /api/admin/users/{id}, links + real_user_traffic ON, max_links=100/max_clicks=100000). Each test uploads a fresh batch of 5 dummy proxies (192.0.2.x:8080:u:p) + 5 Android UAs + (where needed) a 5-row XLSX, fires a small RUT job (concurrency=1, no_repeated_proxy=true, dummy proxies — picks happen BEFORE network call so connectivity not required), polls /api/real-user-traffic/jobs/{id} until terminal, and reads the user-DB uploaded_resources collection directly via motor (the public GET /api/uploads strips items[] / file_path so we bypass it for items verification). Result: 2/5 PASS, 3/5 FAIL — and the 3 failures point at the SAME root-cause defect: the LAST visit's fire-and-forget `asyncio.create_task(_live_remove_proxy(...))` / `_live_remove_ua(...)` task is sometimes lost when run_real_user_traffic_job() reaches _finalise_and_persist() before the in-flight $pull coroutine has a chance to run. consumed_count consistently lands at exactly 2 instead of the expected 3 across two independent test runs (TestLiveProxyRemoval and TestLiveUaRemoval), and the auto-delete edge case (3 proxies, 3 visits → expect doc gone) leaves 1 item behind with consumed_count=2. The 2-visit positive test PASSES (consumed=2, items_left=3), and the endpoint smoke test PASSES (POST /api/real-user-traffic/jobs accepts all three upload_*_id Form fields and returns 200 with the new payload echoed back). Total runtime: 1157s (~19 min) due to job-poll waits.

critical.issue: Fire-and-forget asyncio.create_task(_live_remove_proxy / _live_remove_ua) launched in process_one() can be CANCELLED or never scheduled before run_real_user_traffic_job() finalises. Net effect: with total_clicks=3 the user's saved batch is only $pulled twice (consumed_count=2, items_count=3 instead of 2). Reproduced 3× in this iteration: TestLiveProxyRemoval (3-visit, 5 proxies → 3 left, expected 2), TestLiveUaRemoval (3-visit, 5 UAs → 3 left, expected 2), TestAutoDeleteWhenEmpty (3-visit, 3 proxies → batch NOT auto-deleted, 1 item still present + consumed_count=2). The 2-visit case (TestUnusedItemsRemain) passes — only the FINAL visit's pull is lost. Likely fix: collect each create_task() handle in a job-scoped set + `await asyncio.gather(*pending, return_exceptions=True)` inside _finalise_and_persist BEFORE marking job terminal & returning. Same pattern needed for _live_remove_data_row.

action_items: FIX HIGH: Drain pending live-remove tasks before run_real_user_traffic_job() returns. In real_user_traffic.py around line 1024-1031, replace the bare `asyncio.create_task(...)` calls with task-tracking: keep a set `_pending_live_pulls = set()`; for every create_task add `t.add_done_callback(_pending_live_pulls.discard)`; and in _finalise_and_persist (or just before it) `await asyncio.gather(*_pending_live_pulls, return_exceptions=True)` so the LAST visit's $pull always completes. Otherwise the user sees N-1 lines deleted instead of N for short jobs (and the auto-delete path never triggers cleanly). Same drain needed for _live_remove_data_row.
RE-RUN tests/test_iteration17_per_use_deletion.py after the drain fix — expect 5/5 green.
critical_code_review_comments: real_user_traffic.py:1281 & 1291 — fire-and-forget asyncio.create_task() WITHOUT keeping a strong reference is a known asyncio footgun (CPython docs explicitly warn the task may be garbage-collected mid-flight: https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-task.html#asyncio.create_task). Pair this with the engine reaching `_finalise_and_persist` immediately after the last visit, and the final pull is racing the function return. A task-set + done_callback pattern is the canonical fix; alternatively `await` the pull inline (the user has already accepted the visit blocking on a Mongo round-trip elsewhere — adding ~5-20ms is acceptable).

real_user_traffic.py:1093-1173 — _live_remove_data_row uses an asyncio.Lock to serialise XLSX rewrites, but the same fire-and-forget pattern applies (line 1552 + 1629). When all 3 attempted rows hit on the very last 1-2 visits of a job, the same drain bug will lose the final XLSX rewrite and leave the file with one extra row. NOT covered in this iteration (would require form_fill_enabled=true with a real form that the dummy proxy can submit — out of scope for backend-only) but the same drain fix applies.

real_user_traffic.py:1032 — `user_db_truncated = (engine_user_id or '').replace('-', '_')[:20]` correctly mirrors server.py:92 `get_user_db()` truncation. LGTM. Consider extracting both into a shared `_user_db_name(user_id)` helper so they can never drift.

real_user_traffic.py:1057-1060 / 1086-1089 — the auto-delete check `if doc and not (doc.get('items') or [])` is correct but ONLY runs when `res.modified_count` was non-zero on this exact $pull. If two concurrent pulls race for the last item, only one observes modified_count=1 and runs the delete; the other sees modified_count=0 and bails. Probably fine because the surviving pull will still trigger the delete. Worth a comment though.

server.py:3634-3637 — engine_user_id forwarding looks correct. Note that `(upload_proxy_id or None)` is redundant since Form(None) already produces None for absent fields, but it's defensive against empty-string Form values from the UI — keep as-is.

ENHANCEMENT — surface consumed_count + item_count in the GET /api/uploads response (already done) is great; consider also adding `last_consumed_at` so the user can see live progress in the UI without hitting the job page.

success_rate.backend: 40% (2/5 — 3 failures are all the same root cause: lost trailing live-remove task)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@trackmaster.local / admin123 via POST /api/admin/login. Per-test isolated user TEST_iter17_<hex>@example.com / Pass1234! created via /api/auth/register and torn down via DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} + drop_database(trackmaster_user_<truncated>) in module fixture teardown.
seed_data_creation: Module-scoped fixture creates: (1) one TEST_iter17_<hex>@example.com user, (2) one tracking link `TEST_iter17_link_<hex>` pointing at https://example.com/offer-iter17. Per-test it also creates fresh proxy/UA/data-file uploaded_resources docs in the user's per-user DB. All cleaned up at module teardown (user delete cascades, plus explicit drop_database call).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run after main agent applies the asyncio drain fix: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_iteration17_per_use_deletion.py -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/iteration17_rerun.xml. The full suite takes ~19 min because 4 of 5 tests start a real RUT job and poll until terminal (timeout 240s each). To speed up, the engine could expose a 'fast-path' env flag that skips the chromium preflight when no real visits will succeed — out of scope for now. Note: the FAILURES are real bugs, NOT flaky tests — they reproduce 100% of the time as long as the last visit's $pull is fire-and-forget. Iter15+Iter16 regression NOT re-run this iteration since the engine code-path under test was the new live-remove logic; main agent / next testing agent should run them after the fix to confirm no regression: pytest tests/test_iteration14_rut_engine_status.py tests/test_iteration15_rut_engine_prewarm.py tests/test_iteration16_os_fold_and_captcha.py.
rca of the issue: REAL BUG SURFACED. User asked for 'ek line use hoe wo sath he delete ho jay' (per-use immediate deletion). Main agent implemented `asyncio.create_task(_live_remove_proxy(raw_line))` at real_user_traffic.py:1281 + same for UA at 1291. The pull is correct LOGICALLY but DELIVERY is unreliable: when total_clicks=3 the loop runs through 3 process_one() iterations, each schedules 1 proxy + 1 UA pull as detached tasks. After the 3rd iteration the orchestrator calls _finalise_and_persist (line 916+) which UPDATES the job doc to terminal status and the BackgroundTasks coroutine returns. The 3rd visit's pull tasks have not yet hit the network round-trip — they are scheduled but not awaited — and CPython's task GC + the FastAPI BackgroundTask lifecycle means the third $pull is silently dropped. Empirical proof from this iteration: TestLiveProxyRemoval observed consumed_count=2 (NOT 3) and items_after=3 (NOT 2), TestLiveUaRemoval observed identical pattern, TestAutoDeleteWhenEmpty (3 proxies, 3 visits) observed consumed_count=2 + 1 item left + doc still present. The 2-visit happy path (TestUnusedItemsRemain) PASSED with 3 items left + 2 consumed because 2 of the 2 pulls land — only the LAST iteration's pull is racy. Mitigation: maintain a per-job task-set (e.g. `RUT_JOBS[job_id]['_live_pulls'] = set()`), `add_done_callback(_live_pulls.discard)` on every create_task, and `await asyncio.gather(*_live_pulls, return_exceptions=True)` immediately before _finalise_and_persist marks the job terminal. Apply the same drain to _live_remove_data_row tasks (created at line 1552 + 1629). After the fix, re-run iter17 — expect 5/5 green.
